member.name: Joe Morris

addresses: Queen's Hall Arts Centre, Beaumont Street, Hexham, Northumberland, NE46 3LS

member.synopsis: Joe Morris is the Labour MP for Hexham , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Innovation: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: ?To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to support locally led innovation investment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I’m proud that just last month the Secretary of State announced we will devolve future Local Innovation Partnerships Funding to mayors of Established Mayoral Strategic Authorities.This will put money and power into the hands of regional leaders that know ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the tax system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reforms across the last two Budgets have shown that the government is committed to a fair and efficient tax system. The UK tax system generates substantial revenue to fund our vital public services whilst remaining internationally competitive – in the G7,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Rented Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to improve the private rented sector for tenants.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 1 May, we switched on the first phase of our transformative Renters’ Rights Act. Measures brought into force included the long overdue abolition of Section 21 ‘no fault’ evictions, a limit of one rent increase a year, new rights to challenge unreasonab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cinemas: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, if she will (a) include cinemas as eligible recipients of capital funding for UK cultural venues and (b) introduce a (i) complementary and (ii) parallel funding scheme for cinemas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Cinemas help to sustain our high streets whilst offering one of the most accessible and affordable cultural experiences for communities around the country.In a challenging fiscal environment, we have to make difficult choices about where to direct availab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Arts: North East
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, how her Department plans to support the BBC in growing the North East’s creative economy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The BBC is a vital national institution and an engine of the nations and regions, including the North East of England. In that context, this news is concerning for the BBC workforce who are facing significant uncertainty, and for the sector as a whole. Th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Creative Europe
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether she plans to re-join Creative Europe.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government does not have any plans to rejoin Creative Europe, though we are committed to finding constructive ways to work with the EU and deliver for the British people on shared priorities and global challenges.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Sports: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to support rural sports clubs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting grassroots sport clubs, including those in rural areas, which play an important role within communities across the country. The Government provides the majority of support for grassroots sport clubs through our Ar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicle Drivers: Facilities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the availability of quality roadside facilities on recruitment and retention of HGV drivers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the Rapid Evidence Assessment published in October 2023, the Department has assessed the potential impact of the availability and quality of roadside facilities on HGV driver recruitment and retention, finding that inadequate facilities can act as...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she has taken with Cabinet colleagues to increase local roads maintenance funding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed a record level of funding for local authorities to repair, renew and fix potholes; totalling over £2 billion annually by 2029-30. This is double the amount provided by the previous government – and it ensures that we will exce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Cinemas: Government Assistance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what opportunities her department has for independent cinemas to apply for national support schemes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Independent cinemas play an important role in our communities, providing an accessible form of culture for people across the UK. Independent cinemas can apply for various public funding schemes through DCMS's Arm's Length Bodies if they meet the eligibili...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to supporting the aspiration of every person who meets the requirements and wants to attend higher education.Targeted maintenance grants from the academic year 2028/29 onwards will provide up to £1,000 extra support per year t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Science and Discovery Centres

writtenQuestions.answerText: Science and Discovery Centres are primarily cultural institutions and visitor attractions which would normally fall under the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport (DCMS) remit.Where they carry out research, they are funded for those activities...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Principles of Engagement was published under the 2022 to 2024 Conservative government. Responsibility for decisions and due diligence around who departments engage with sits with those departments and the appropriate policy areas.It is for ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Moorland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to mitigate the loss and fragmentation of open moorland habitats across the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moorland areas contain a wide variety of habitats and species of importance. We are committed, through grant programmes such as Landscape Recovery, Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier, the Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI) and Farming in Protected Lands...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to provide reliable broadband service to rural areas that fall into the category of ‘Very Hard to Reach’, and outside of the scope of contracts already awarded
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through a combination of commercial market and government subsidised delivery, government is ensuring 99% of UK premises will have access to gigabit-capable broadband by 2032. However, our expectation is that some remote premises will remain too expensive...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Culture: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: In January 2026, the Government announced a package of £1.5 billion Arts Everywhere funding to support arts, culture libraries and heritage over the course of this parliament. The vast majority of this funding is capital investment, and is the single bigg...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Supply: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps are being taken to ensure the enforcement of regular risk assessment and monitoring of private water supplies by local authorities as per their responsibilities outlined in
writtenQuestions.answerText: Private water supplies are regulated by local authorities. They are responsible for identifying risks to the quality of the water and must have comprehensive monitoring programmes in place to sample the drinking water for any element, organism or substanc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Classics: State Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has plans to increase the number of state school students able to study Latin and the Classics.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to supporting a broad and balanced curriculum that is inclusive and accessible to all pupils. The study of classics and ancient languages can teach pupils valuable lessons in history, politics and art that are still relevant to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Unemployment: North East
